'''Jake Bibby''' (born 17 June 1996) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. Bibby spent time on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

==Background==
Bibby was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester, England]].

==Playing career==
===Salford===
Bibby made his Salford début in a Super League Super 8s match away at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 27 September 2015.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Jake-Bibby/|title = Players}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Halifax===
He has made 3 appearances on Dual Registration for Halifax RLFC in 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-jake-bibby-salfords-powerful-10431937/|title=Meet Jake Bibby - Salford's powerful three-quarter who overcame early nerves to thrive with Red Devils|website=www.mirror.co.uk|access-date=16 November 2020|archive-date=12 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180212022410/https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/meet-jake-bibby-salfords-powerful-10431937|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
Bibby moved to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] ahead of the [[2020 Super League season]]. He played in the club's 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] loss against St Helens at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.  Bibby scored a try for Wigan in the second half of the game.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2021 Super League season, he scored two tries for Wigan in a 20-18 victory over Leigh.  The following week, he scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 34-6 victory over Wakefield.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56576124|title=Wigan 34-6 Wakefield - Jake Bibby hat-trick helps Warriors win|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
In round 6 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for Wigan in a 30-16 victory over Leigh.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57128147|title=Super League: Leigh 16-30 Wigan - Warriors rally to beat neighbours|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Bibby scored two tries in a 24-10 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/match-report-hull-kr-wigan-warriors-3565355/|title=Wigan Warriors start their Super League campaign with a 24-10 win away to Hull KR|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Bibby played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 28 September 2022, Bibby signed a three-year deal with the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63058829|title=Huddersfield sign Jake Bibby from Wigan|date=28 September 2022|work=BBC Sport|access-date=12 October 2022}}</ref>
Bibby played 17 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com|date=29 September 2023 }}</ref>
Bibby played 17 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season scoring seven tries as the club finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
Bibby played 26 games for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 31 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 Oct 2025|accessdate=10 Jan 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Released Super League stalwart lands deal with Championship heavyweights for 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jake-bibby-joins-oldham-for-2026}}</ref>
